(B) trells This changes the first letter "t" to "tr" and removes the second "o." This makes "trells," which is a real word (a structure for training plants). It's not really a humorous variation of "trolls," though; it's just a different word. (C) trollb This changes the last "s" to "b." It gives the word a slightly awkward or silly sound, which could be seen as humorous. It's similar to the original but with a twist. (D) trills This changes the "o" to "i," making "trills." This is a real word, meaning to sing or play with a trill (a musical ornament). This is a clever variation since it's a real, pronounceable word and sounds similar to "trolls." It also has a musical connotation, which might be funny in the context of the movie "Trolls" if you think of the characters singing or making music. Based on my analysis, (D) trills seems like the most humorous and clever edit of the word "trolls."
